result:
The `{area}:calfence:{bx}:{by}` tokens are intentional f-string code in the implementation brief, not leftovers. The pre-registration is complete. My work as the design iteration is done — the harness takes it from here.

## Summary

**Pre-registered experiment 32** (`runs/pending_experiment.json`, figcheck PASS): **Risk-controlled abstention — six per-lighting-regime confidence thresholds, calibrated on never-trained fenced blocks.** Category: training; changes the selective-prediction path only, everything else byte-identical.

**Why this pivot.** Before designing, I ran three read-only probes of the champion plus forensics on the six reverted experiments, and they rewrote the story of the plateau:

1. **The last four "refutations" were largely measurement artifacts.** Exps 28–31 all ran at much higher coverage than the champion and each was killed by one night/evening cell. Exp 28 (3× trunk) beat or matched the champion in 17 of 24 cells at coverage 0.52–0.79 (vs 0.42–0.67) and lost solely on berlin night, where its single global threshold kept 44.5% of crops. The §6 metric is median-over-kept, and the current calibration (one global shift, min-across-buckets, tuned on train-visited ground) leaves each cell's operating point essentially accidental — the champion wins partly by abstaining harder by luck.
2. **Ruled out cheaper stories with probes:** rotation invariance is solved (error flat vs residual heading mismatch); decode is not the problem (argmax ≈ window ≈ exported decode; true cell is top-1 on only 2% of crops — ranking quality is the real wall, which no quick fix addresses). A bonus discovery: the model is hypersensitive to resampling blur (crisp crops double the error) — logged to memory as a future lever.
3. **Quantified the headroom:** confidence ranks errors in every cell (Spearman −0.21…−0.42); at per-cell-optimal keep rates the worst §6 cell drops from 797.9 → 731.1 m oracle.

**The design:** fence ~10% of train blocks (stable-hash, bbox-generic, same halo rule as eval blocks) out of every training draw; after training, trace each lighting bucket's risk–coverage curve on those honestly-unseen blocks and pick its keep rate (grid floor 0.26, guarded by exp 28's measured upward-biased fenced→eval transfer); bake six (luminance, threshold) anchors into ONNX buffers, interpolated at inference from the frame's own brightness — no bucket label needed in flight.

**Honest prediction:** primary ~745–795 m (0.5–7% better; point estimate ~765 m). The pre-registered secondary value is instrument repair: with controlled operating points, future representation gains (exp 28's trunk being the obvious retest) stop being vetoed by threshold flukes. Memory updated accordingly.

tool_input.description: Run heading-mismatch probe with repo on PYTHONPATH
Run heading-mismatch probe on champion model
